verses.text: God makes Moses 'a god to Pharaoh,' with Aaron as his prophet to speak. Empowered. God's servant goes with divine authority.
God makes Moses 'a god to Pharaoh,' with Aaron as his prophet to speak. Empowered. God's servant goes with divine authority.
God will harden Pharaoh and multiply signs, that Egypt 'shall know that I am the LORD.' Known by judgment. God's glory is the aim.
God will harden Pharaoh and multiply signs, that Egypt 'shall know that I am the LORD.' Known by judgment. God's glory is the aim.
God will harden Pharaoh and multiply signs, that Egypt 'shall know that I am the LORD.' Known by judgment. God's glory is the aim.
Moses and Aaron obey; Moses is eighty, Aaron eighty-three. Obedient elders. Age is no bar to God's service.
Moses and Aaron obey; Moses is eighty, Aaron eighty-three. Obedient elders. Age is no bar to God's service.
Before Pharaoh, Aaron's rod becomes a serpent. The sign. God gives proof of His sending.
Before Pharaoh, Aaron's rod becomes a serpent. The sign. God gives proof of His sending.
Before Pharaoh, Aaron's rod becomes a serpent. The sign. God gives proof of His sending.
The magicians do likewise, but Aaron's rod swallows theirs; still his heart hardens. Swallowed. God's power outdoes the counterfeit, yet pride resists.
The magicians do likewise, but Aaron's rod swallows theirs; still his heart hardens. Swallowed. God's power outdoes the counterfeit, yet pride resists.
The magicians do likewise, but Aaron's rod swallows theirs; still his heart hardens. Swallowed. God's power outdoes the counterfeit, yet pride resists.
The first plague is announced: the river shall turn to blood, the fish die. Water to blood. Egypt's god, the Nile, is struck first.
The first plague is announced: the river shall turn to blood, the fish die. Water to blood. Egypt's god, the Nile, is struck first.

The first plague is announced: the river shall turn to blood, the fish die. Water to blood. Egypt's god, the Nile, is struck first.
The first plague is announced: the river shall turn to blood, the fish die. Water to blood. Egypt's god, the Nile, is struck first.
Aaron smites the waters; all Egypt's rivers become blood and the fish die. Fulfilled. The land's lifeblood is poisoned.
Aaron smites the waters; all Egypt's rivers become blood and the fish die. Fulfilled. The land's lifeblood is poisoned.
Aaron smites the waters; all Egypt's rivers become blood and the fish die. Fulfilled. The land's lifeblood is poisoned.
The magicians imitate it; Pharaoh's heart is hardened, and seven days pass. Unmoved. Even a stinking land does not bend the king.
